title: Rewovability Operator
canonical_statement: Viability is the rewovability of regenerative accessibility fibers.
definition: The rewovability operator estimates whether fragmented recoverable accessibility fibers can restore admissible transformational continuity under repair dynamics.

output_interpretation.\\mathcal W_{\\mathrm{reweave}}\\to0: Regenerative accessibility fibers can no longer be rewoven.

collapse_condition: Collapse occurs when regenerative accessibility fibers can no longer be rewoven.
open_formalization_items: Define repair dynamics acting on fragmented accessibility fibers.
one_sentence_lock: The rewovability operator measures whether regenerative accessibility fibers can be reconnected after fragmentation.
